---
title: "Math AI RCT — Data Analysis Report"
subtitle: "Overview & Recommendations"
author: "Jhony Pulido"
date: today
---

## Study Context

This report analyzes data from a randomized controlled trial (RCT) evaluating the impact of an AI-based math tutor on learning outcomes and post-secondary decisions. The study was conducted across **30 secondary schools** in Lima, Peru (DRELM), with approximately **1,200 fifth-year students** assigned to three arms:

- **Control** — no intervention
- **T1** — Standard AI Tutor (uDocz platform)
- **T2** — Modified AI Tutor (uDocz platform with additional features)

Randomization was at the **school level**. Data come from a Qualtrics baseline survey administered before treatment, and interaction logs from the uDocz educational platform.

## Report Sections

| Section | Focus |
|---|---|
| [Data Quality](01_data_quality.qmd) | Missing values, validity checks, ID coverage across datasets |
| [Descriptives](02_descriptives.qmd) | Sample characteristics, platform engagement, math performance |
| [Balance Tests](03_balance.qmd) | Covariate balance across treatment arms, joint orthogonality test |

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-note}
This section will be completed after all analyses are run. It will summarize the most important findings from each section and their implications.
:::

### Data Quality
*To be completed.*

### Descriptive Patterns
*To be completed.*

### Balance Assessment
*To be completed.*

## Recommendations

::: {.callout-note}
This section will be completed after all analyses are run.
:::

Key questions to address:

- Which variables have quality issues that require attention in downstream analysis?
- Is attrition from baseline to platform usage differential across treatment arms?
- Does the balance assessment suggest the need for covariate adjustment in outcome regressions?
- Which subsamples or variables should be prioritized or excluded in impact evaluations?
